summary: This is a brief link roundup post where Joel shares three items: a review calling the Grand Canyon Skywalk a ripoff, a podcast interview he did about customer service and user experience, and a travel anecdote about a miserable experience changing planes at Paris Charles de Gaulle airport via Air France. The post is extremely short and informal, serving more as a personal update than a substantive essay.
key_points: The Grand Canyon Skywalk is characterized as a tourist trap
Joel was interviewed about customer service for a UX podcast, suggesting his interest in user experience topics
Charles de Gaulle airport is singled out as a terrible experience for connecting flights, with Joel noting that even routine scheduled arrivals seem to surprise the ground crew
money_quotes: Nothing surprises the ground crew at CDG more than the arrival of the daily, scheduled flight from Kennedy.
Every single thing about that airport is broken and it's the worst place in the world to change planes.
key_insight: A lightweight link post with no central thesis, though Joel's CDG rant reflects his characteristic intolerance for broken systems and poor user experiences.
